title: poa ochotensis subsp. stepposa
intro: Poa ochotensis subsp. stepposa, also known as steppes meadow-grass, is a subspecies of the Poa ochotensis grass native to the mountainous regions of East Asia. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, attributed to its saponin and flavonoid content. Steppes meadow-grass has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, such as fever, cough, and gastrointestinal problems, due to its ability to stimulate digestion and relieve inflammation. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in teas, infusions, and decoctions, which are believed to promote overall well-being and provide relief from respiratory and digestive issues.
